title: Mustardy Infused Veal Blanquette
description: A classic French veal blanquette infused with the warmth of mustard and the salty sweetness of serrano ham.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together the flour and a pinch of salt.
Season the veal with salt and pepper.
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the veal to the saucepan and sear until browned, about 2 minutes per side.
Transfer the veal to a plate and set aside.
In the same saucepan, add another 2 tablespoons of butter.
Add the serrano ham to the saucepan and cook until slightly caramelized, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, white wine, and mustard to the saucepan, whisking to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, or until slightly reduced.
Return the veal to the saucepan and simmer for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until cooked through.
Stir in the milk and cook over low heat until the sauce has thickened.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan.
Return the veal to the saucepan and stir in the parsley and mustardy oil.
Serve the veal hot, spooning some of the sauce over the top.
